https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Ujigami Shrine]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|qid=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|qid=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}}

The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}}

a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] {{draft categories| }} [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]

a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] as one of the "[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]]". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasures]] in the [[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|Category shrines]].{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]

Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital [[:en:Dendrochronology|Dendrochronology]] to be the oldest original [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *[[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)]] *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
