[[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q583589]] Wikidata name: Ujigami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology|1=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology|1=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ujigami Jinja]][[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]][[de:Ujigami-Schrein]][[en:Ujigami Shrine]][[es:Santuario Ujigami]][[eu:Ujigami santutegia]][[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]][[fi:Ujigami-jinja]][[fr:Ujigami-jinja]][[hy:Ուձիգամի]][[ja:宇治上神社]][[ko:우지가미 신사]][[la:Templum Ujigami]][[pl:Ujigami-jinja]][[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]][[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]][[ru:Удзигами]][[tr:Ujigami Tapınağı]][[zh:宇治上神社]] {{infobox religious building | name = Ujigami Shrine
宇治上神社 | image = File:Ujigami-jinja, honden.jpg | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' main hall of the Ujigami Shrine,
a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.892067|N|135.811439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Minor ''shikinaisha'' | deity = {{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded_by = | established = 11th century | date_destroyed = | location = 59 Ujiyamada, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}} | part_of = {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto|WD=Q874027|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Ujigami Shrine'''|宇治上神社|Ujigami-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine was built as a guardian shrine for the nearby [[Byōdō-in]], and is adjacent to the [[Uji Shrine]]. In 1994, it was registered as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} as one of the "{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}}". The ''[[honden]]'' and ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' have been designated by the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] as {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasures|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=Category shrines|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}}.{{Cite web | last = The Agency for Cultural Affairs | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | publisher = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-12-15 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2005-12-28 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20051228033527/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru daijisen | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite web | url = http://www.culturalprofiles.net/japan/units/4552.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | year = 2006 | publisher = Japan Cultural Profile | location = Cambridge, United Kingdom | access-date = 2012-05-28}}{{cite web | url = http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | title = Ujigami Shrine | year = 1996 | publisher = Kansai Digital Archives | location = Kita-ku, Osaka, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | archive-date = 2012-02-11 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120211232140/http://www.kiis.or.jp/kansaida/uji/uji02-e.html | url-status = dead }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = Ujigami-jinja | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-05-28 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }} The Ujigami Shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and his sons, the imperial princes [[Uji no Wakiiratsuko]] and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. Uji no Wakiiratsuko committed suicide to solve a dispute over the imperial succession, and the shrine was built in his honor.{{cite web | url = http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | title = Ujigami-jinja Shrine | publisher = Kyoto Convention Bureau | location = Kyoto, Japan | access-date = 2012-05-28 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120523152426/http://www.kyoto.travel/2009/11/ujigami-jinja-shrine.html | archive-date = 2012-05-23 }} The [[honden]] of the Ujigami Shrine is known as the oldest example of ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style of shrine architecture in Japan. In this style of architecture the three inner shrine structures are built side-by-side, with the structure in the middle being larger than those to the left and right. The honden dates to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} (794 – 1185). The haiden is built in the ''shinden-zukuri'' style, and its roof in the ''sugaruhafu'' style. The haiden dates to the Kamakura period (1185 – 1333). The Kasuga Shrine, also within the shrine precinct, dates to the same period. The shrine is noted for its freshwater spring. Ujigami Shrine was found via digital {{ill|Dendrochronology|en|Dendrochronology|WD=Q80205|lt=Dendrochronology|1=Dendrochronology}} to be the oldest original {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. The [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] determined that the shrine was built in approximately 1060, which closely matches the written account of the founding of the shrine.{{cite web | url = http://repository.nabunken.go.jp/modules/xoonips/download.php?file_id=335 | script-title = ja:埋蔵文化財センター 古環境研究室 | publisher = Research Information Repository, Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute | location = Nara, Nara Prefecture, Japan | language = ja | access-date = 2012-05-28 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=no }} Until the Meiji Period (1868 – 1912) the Uji and Ujigami shrines were collectively known as the Rikyukamisha. The annual festival of the Ujigami Shrine is held on May 5. [[File:Ujigami shrine.jpg|thumb|Ujigami shrine]] ==See also== *[[Uji Shrine]] *[[Ujigami]] *{{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|WD=Q874027|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Kyoto (Kyoto, Uji and Otsu Cities)}} *[[Byōdō-in]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Ujigami Jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{World Heritage Kyoto}} ‎]] [[en:Ujigami Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujigami Jinja}} [[cs:Svatyně Udžigami]] [[de:Ujigami-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Ujigami]] [[eu:Ujigami santutegia]] [[fa:معبد اوجیگامی]] [[fi:Ujigami-jinja]] [[fr:Ujigami-jinja]] [[hy:Ուձիգամի]] [[ja:宇治上神社]] [[ko:우지가미 신사]] [[la:Templum Ujigami]] [[pl:Ujigami-jinja]] [[pt:Santuário de Ujigami]] [[ro:Ujigami (sanctuar)]] [[ru:Удзигами]] [[zh:宇治上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q583589}} [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
